name: eugenol
contraindications: Generally safe at dietary levels, but high-dose exposure may increase liver-toxicity risk.
May enhance anticoagulant effects or bleeding tendency in susceptible settings.
description: eugenol appears in records as a compound reported in nan, pimenta dioica, and related herbs. Current source coverage is limited and still developing.
summary: eugenol appears in records as a compound reported in nan, pimenta dioica, and related herbs. Current source coverage is limited and still developing.
mechanism: Exhibits analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects through prostaglandin-pathway inhibition and ion-channel modulation including TRPV1-related activity; also shows antimicrobial activity.
